title: Dorothy Charach
content:
Partner | Health Industry Group Co-Leader
People.ContactEmail dcharach@mccarthy.ca
- Osgoode Hall Law School
Dorothy is known for her keen ability to hone in on the crux of her clients’ issues, advocating effectively for their needs and delivering results both in and out of the courtroom.
Dorothy is a partner in our Litigation Group in Toronto and Co-leader of our Health Industry Group . She maintains a broad practice with an emphasis on commercial litigation and dispute resolution, professional negligence, product liability, class actions, and defamation. She has appeared before all levels of court in Ontario and the Supreme Court of Canada. She has experience serving as lead counsel at trial and on appeal.
Dorothy regularly acts for companies, including manufacturers, distributors, and retailers in commercial disputes such as contractual claims, product liability matters, and class actions. She also has considerable experience advising corporations and individuals on matters related to defending their reputations. In her professional negligence practice, she has successfully defended multiple physicians in several professional negligence cases tried by jury and by judge alone.
Dorothy’s experience includes:
- Acting for defendants in class action matters, including: Successfully resisting certification of a class action on behalf of a pharmaceutical company in relation to an anti-depressant medication alleged to cause birth defects to expectant mothers; Successfully resisting certification of a class action on behalf of a marketer and distributor of spirits and wines in relation to allegations of negligence and negligent misrepresentation for ethanol emissions alleged to cause fungal growth; Obtaining favourable settlements for clients in class action matters, including pharmaceutical and medical device manufacturers;
- Successfully resisting certification of a class action on behalf of a pharmaceutical company in relation to an anti-depressant medication alleged to cause birth defects to expectant mothers;
- Successfully resisting certification of a class action on behalf of a marketer and distributor of spirits and wines in relation to allegations of negligence and negligent misrepresentation for ethanol emissions alleged to cause fungal growth;
- Obtaining favourable settlements for clients in class action matters, including pharmaceutical and medical device manufacturers;
- Acting for companies in commercial disputes, including on behalf of: A leading technology company in a significant lease dispute; A leading food and beverage manufacturer in a dispute involving the alleged negligent supply of contaminated products; A prominent retailer in an environmental tort claim involving an alleged historical hydrocarbon spill; Notable pharmaceutical and technology companies in contractual disputes;
- A leading technology company in a significant lease dispute;
- A leading food and beverage manufacturer in a dispute involving the alleged negligent supply of contaminated products;
- A prominent retailer in an environmental tort claim involving an alleged historical hydrocarbon spill;
- Notable pharmaceutical and technology companies in contractual disputes;
- Acting for clients in defamation matters, including: Successfully resisting an anti-SLAPP motion and obtaining summary judgment for an expert defamed on a ratings website (upheld on appeal); Successfully resisting an anti-SLAPP motion on behalf of a hospitality company in a defamation action against a Union and its representatives; On behalf of the former CEO of a large public company in a claims hearing under the Companies’ Creditors Arrangement Act ; Multiple companies and individuals to assist them to remove and/or avoid defamatory publications;
- Successfully resisting an anti-SLAPP motion and obtaining summary judgment for an expert defamed on a ratings website (upheld on appeal);
- Successfully resisting an anti-SLAPP motion on behalf of a hospitality company in a defamation action against a Union and its representatives;
- On behalf of the former CEO of a large public company in a claims hearing under the Companies’ Creditors Arrangement Act ;
- Multiple companies and individuals to assist them to remove and/or avoid defamatory publications;
- Acting for clients on procedural motions, including: Having a motion for the extension of time for service of a Statement of Claim dismissed such that the action was unable to continue (upheld on appeal); Successfully resisting a motion to stay a significant portion of a technology company’s claim on the basis of a forum selection clause in favour of another jurisdiction; Obtaining a stay of an Application in the Ontario Superior Court based on the doctrine of forum non conveniens on behalf of a retailer in a contract dispute; and Successfully obtaining multiple Norwich Orders on Applications for information in situations where individuals and companies were wronged by anonymous tortfeasors, including through defamation and breach of contract/confidence.
- Having a motion for the extension of time for service of a Statement of Claim dismissed such that the action was unable to continue (upheld on appeal);
- Successfully resisting a motion to stay a significant portion of a technology company’s claim on the basis of a forum selection clause in favour of another jurisdiction;
- Obtaining a stay of an Application in the Ontario Superior Court based on the doctrine of forum non conveniens on behalf of a retailer in a contract dispute; and
- Successfully obtaining multiple Norwich Orders on Applications for information in situations where individuals and companies were wronged by anonymous tortfeasors, including through defamation and breach of contract/confidence.
Dorothy is a member of the teaching team for the Trial Advocacy course at Osgoode Hall Law School, and a previous member of the Lawyer as Negotiator teaching team. She also teaches at Queen’s University Faculty of Law and has guest-lectured at the University of Toronto and Osgoode Hall Professional Development programs, primarily in the area of professional negligence. She is a contributing author to the fifth edition of Defending Class Actions in Canada , published by Lexis Nexis.
Dorothy is also active in the community. She is a Board Member of West Neighbourhood House and regularly assists with pro bono matters.
Dorothy completed her BA (Hons.) in English and Psychology in 2008 from the University of Western Ontario. She received her JD from Osgoode Hall Law School in 2011. While attending law school, she won the Arnup Cup Trial Advocacy Moot and received a number of awards, including a Dean’s Gold Key Award. She was also a Division Leader at the Community and Legal Aid Services Program.
Dorothy was called to the Ontario Bar in 2012. She is a member of the Law Society of Ontario, the Canadian Bar Association, the Ontario Bar Association, and The Advocates’ Society.
